title: 1. Test Live Stream (Default)

action.description: Select a file and stream its metadata to the shared terminal.

options.openLabel: Select any file for live stream test

command.windows: cmd /c echo [STREAM][WIN] Path=${selectFileForStream.path}, Dir=${selectFileForStream.dir}, Name=${selectFileForStream.name}, NameOnly=${selectFileForStream.fileNameOnly}, Ext=${selectFileForStream.fileExt}

successMessage: Live stream test completed. Check the terminal output.
failMessage: Live stream test failed. Please try again.

action.description: Capture command output and save it as a file in the workspace.

options.openLabel: Select any file for file capture test

tasks.command: echo [CAPTURED FOR FILE] Path=${selectFileForFile.path}, Dir=${selectFileForFile.dir}, Name=${selectFileForFile.name}, NameOnly=${selectFileForFile.fileNameOnly}, Ext=${selectFileForFile.fileExt}

action.description: Capture command output and display it in a temporary editor document.

options.openLabel: Select any file for editor capture test

tasks.command: echo [CAPTURED FOR EDITOR] Path=${selectFileForEditor.path}, Dir=${selectFileForEditor.dir}, Name=${selectFileForEditor.name}, NameOnly=${selectFileForEditor.fileNameOnly}, Ext=${selectFileForEditor.fileExt}

action.description: Unzip a firmware archive and run the parser on the extracted contents.
Run the firmware parser on an already extracted firmware folder.
Compress a selected folder into a firmware ZIP archive.
Demonstrate the built-in string manipulation helper functions.

tasks.command: echo Directory: ${string_dir.output} && echo Filename: ${string_basename.output} && echo Filename without extension: ${string_basename_no_ext.output} && echo Extension: ${string_extension.output} && echo Uppercase filename: ${string_uppercase_name.output}

action.description: Attempt to save captured output to an existing file without overwrite and observe the failure.

tasks.command: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const path=require('path'); const target=process.argv[1]; fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(target),{recursive:true}); fs.writeFileSync(target,'Existing content');" "${workspaceFolder}/.vscode/taskhub_overwrite_fail.txt"
echo Trying to save new content without overwrite

action.description: Demonstrate using the overwrite flag to replace an existing output file.

tasks.command: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const path=require('path'); const target=process.argv[1]; fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(target),{recursive:true}); fs.writeFileSync(target,'Old content');" "${workspaceFolder}/.vscode/taskhub_overwrite_success.txt"
echo Replacement content saved with overwrite true
node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const target=process.argv[1]; console.log('Final file contents:', fs.readFileSync(target,'utf8'));" "${workspaceFolder}/.vscode/taskhub_overwrite_success.txt"

action.description: Create an archive and extract it using both inputs-based and direct destination options.

options.openLabel: Select folder to archive for unzip test

tasks.command: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const path=require('path'); const base=process.argv[1]; const targets=[path.join(base,'.vscode','taskhub_unzip_inputs.zip'), path.join(base,'.vscode','taskhub_unzip_destination'), path.join(base,'.vscode','taskhub_unzip_direct')]; for (const target of targets) { if (fs.existsSync(target)) { const stat=fs.statSync(target); if (stat.isDirectory()) { fs.rmSync(target,{recursive:true,force:true}); } else { fs.unlinkSync(target); } } }" "${workspaceFolder}"

node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const path=require('path'); const base=process.argv[1]; const target=path.join(base,'.vscode','taskhub_unzip_destination'); fs.mkdirSync(target,{recursive:true}); process.stdout.write(target);" "${workspaceFolder}"

node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const path=require('path'); const base=process.argv[1]; const destA=path.join(base,'.vscode','taskhub_unzip_destination'); const destB=path.join(base,'.vscode','taskhub_unzip_direct'); const listDir = dir => fs.existsSync(dir) ? fs.readdirSync(dir) : []; console.log('Destination (inputs):', destA); console.log(listDir(destA)); console.log('Destination (direct):', destB); console.log(listDir(destB));" "${workspaceFolder}"

action.description: Demonstrate the default behavior where the terminal is always revealed.

tasks.command: echo 'This terminal should always be revealed.'

action.description: Run a task without revealing the terminal unless it is already visible.

tasks.command: echo 'This task should run silently without revealing the terminal unless it was already open.'

action.description: Run a task that keeps the terminal hidden even while it executes.

tasks.command: echo 'This task should run without ever revealing the terminal.'

action.description: Show how a one-shot background task runs alongside immediate follow-up steps.

tasks.command: echo 'Starting 10-second background task...'; sleep 10; echo 'Background task finished.'
echo 'This message should appear immediately, without waiting for the 10-second task to finish.'

action.description: Illustrate multiple tasks writing to the same shared terminal instance.

tasks.command: echo 'Task 1: This should appear in the shared terminal.'
echo 'Task 2: This should appear in the SAME shared terminal.'
